audit.aligned: - SessionStart: two entries — blank matcher (session-start.sh, injects PATH/mise/fnm/venv via CLAUDE_ENV_FILE, emits git context) and "compact" matcher (inline git log reinject). Matches docs' recommended compact-reinject pattern exactly. - PreCompact: wired to pre-compact.sh, logs trigger + session_id to compaction.log. Correct event name and structure. - PostCompact: wired to post-compact.sh, logs + bell alert. Correct event name. - WorktreeCreate + WorktreeRemove: both wired to worktree-lifecycle.sh, which dispatches on hook_event_name — correct dual-event pattern. - StopFailure: wired to stop-failure.sh, logs error and fires osascript notification with sanitised error string. - SessionEnd: wired to session-lifecycle.sh, logs session_id + cwd. - Notification: wired with "idle_prompt" matcher for osascript desktop notification. - ConfigChange: wired to config-change.sh, appends tab-separated audit rows to config-audit.log. - PermissionRequest: wired with "ExitPlanMode" matcher, returns structured allow JSON — matches docs example verbatim. - Stop: wired to stop.sh, logs turn completions. - PostToolUse: wired to post-tool-use-file-audit.sh with "Edit|Write" matcher — logs all file mutations. Matches docs auto-format pattern (different purpose, same structure). - hooks/hooks.json (plugin scope): SessionStart, PreToolUse (Read|Edit|Write|Bash → access-guard.sh), CwdChanged (→ session-init.sh) — correct use of CwdChanged for environment reload as documented. - Hook scripts all use stdin-read pattern (INPUT=$(cat)) and jq for JSON parsing — matches docs guidance. - stop.sh does NOT have stop_hook_active guard, but it only logs (exit 0 unconditionally) so it cannot create an infinite loop — safe in current form. - ConfigChange matcher left blank (fires on all config source types) — valid per docs. - Hook scripts use stderr for messages where blocking would be relevant (worktree-lifecycle.sh pattern is correct). - settings.json uses disableAllHooks-compatible structure (hooks block at root).
audit.gaps:
1. UserPromptSubmit — not wired. Fires before Claude processes a prompt. Useful for injecting additionalContext (audit log of all prompts, rate-limit guard, prompt prefix injection).
2. PostToolUseFailure — not wired. Fires after a tool call fails. Currently failures are only captured via StopFailure (API-level). Tool-level failures (e.g. failed Bash command, bad file path) go unlogged.
3. SubagentStart — not wired. CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS is enabled in env, making this relevant. No logging or control of subagent spawning.
4. SubagentStop — not wired. Companion to SubagentStart. No post-subagent audit.
5. TeammateIdle — not wired. Relevant because agent teams are enabled (CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1). Fires when a teammate is about to go idle; useful for reassigning work or notifying.
6. TaskCompleted — not wired. CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TASKS=true is set. No hook fires when a task completes.
7. InstructionsLoaded — not wired. Fires when CLAUDE.md or rules/*.md files are loaded. Could be used to validate or log which instruction sets are active.
8. FileChanged — not wired. No watched-file hooks for .envrc, .env, or config files. The CwdChanged hook calls session-init.sh but FileChanged would allow finer-grained env reload without a full directory change.
9. Elicitation / ElicitationResult — not wired. No MCP elicitation handling. If MCP servers (neon-postgres, pyright-lsp, netlify-skills) ever request user input mid-call, no hook intercepts or logs it.
10. hooks/hooks.json "TaskCreated" event — this event does NOT exist in the docs event table. The valid event for task lifecycle is TaskCompleted, not TaskCreated. This hook will silently never fire.
11. No hook uses type "prompt" (LLM-evaluated) or type "agent" (subagent with tools) — both documented hook types are unused. Prompt hooks on Stop would enable automatic task-completion verification.
12. No hook uses type "http" — documented for team audit services and external webhooks. Given INTEGRATIONS.md describes a Slack/CI notification architecture, HTTP hooks could replace the shell notify.sh pattern.
13. Notification hook only covers "idle_prompt" matcher — docs list additional notification types: "permission_prompt", "auth_success", "elicitation_dialog". These fire different events and would benefit from distinct notifications or logging.
14. StopFailure matcher left blank (fires on all error types) but stop-failure.sh sends a generic notification for all errors. Docs list specific matchers: rate_limit, authentication_failed, billing_error, invalid_request, server_error, max_output_tokens, unknown. Different handling per error type (e.g. rate_limit → backoff hint; billing_error → urgent alert) is not implemented.
15. PreToolUse hook (access-guard.sh) in hooks.json: the script path is hardcoded to /Users/alexzh/jadecli-ecosystem/scripts/access-guard.sh — not using $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR as docs recommend. Portability gap.
16. SessionStart matcher "compact" (inline inline git log) duplicates logic already in session-start.sh (which also outputs git context). Two hooks for the same event/purpose risk double-injection on compact resume.
audit.actions:
1. ADD PostToolUseFailure hook to settings.json: Create bootstrap/claude-config/hooks/post-tool-use-failure.sh — Log tool_name + error to ~/.claude/logs/tool-failures.log — Wire in settings.json under PostToolUseFailure with matcher ""
2. ADD UserPromptSubmit hook to settings.json: Create bootstrap/claude-config/hooks/user-prompt-submit.sh — Append prompt hash + session_id to ~/.claude/logs/prompt-audit.log (no full text for privacy) — Wire with matcher "" and type "command"
3. ADD SubagentStart + SubagentStop hooks to settings.json: Create bootstrap/claude-config/hooks/subagent-lifecycle.sh (dispatch on hook_event_name like worktree-lifecycle.sh) — Log agent type + session_id — Wire both events to the same script
4. ADD TaskCompleted hook to settings.json: Create bootstrap/claude-config/hooks/task-completed.sh — Log task subject/id to ~/.claude/logs/tasks.log — Optionally fire osascript notification
5. FIX hooks/hooks.json: rename "TaskCreated" to "TaskCompleted" (or remove if TaskCompleted hook above covers it). TaskCreated does not exist in the documented event schema and will never fire.
6. ADD FileChanged hook to settings.json for env reload: { "matcher": ".envrc|.env", "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "direnv export bash >> \"$CLAUDE_ENV_FILE\"" }] } This is a verbatim doc example and complements the existing CwdChanged hook.
7. REVIEW SessionStart duplicate: either remove the inline "compact" matcher entry from settings.json (since session-start.sh already emits git context and will fire on all SessionStart including compact) or guard session-start.sh with a source check to avoid double-injection.
8. FIX hooks/hooks.json hardcoded path: replace /Users/alexzh/jadecli-ecosystem with $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR or a relative reference.
9. CONSIDER adding Notification coverage for "permission_prompt" and "auth_success" matchers with distinct osascript subtitles so permission prompts are distinguishable from idle alerts.
10. CONSIDER a Stop hook with type "prompt" for task-completion verification as shown in the docs — low cost (Haiku), high value for unattended runs.

audit.aligned: - INTEGRATIONS.md documents all four channel types covered by docs: iMessage, Telegram, Discord, and custom webhook. Architecture matches the docs channel model (MCP servers pushing events into running sessions). - INTEGRATIONS.md correctly documents the permission relay feature (v2.1.81+) with the "Reply yes/no <id>" syntax — aligns with docs' relay-permission-prompts capability. - INTEGRATIONS.md references --channels flag and the plugin install flow (/plugin install telegram@claude-plugins-official). - INTEGRATIONS.md correctly places managed settings reference at 10-jadecli-channels.json. - INTEGRATIONS.md documents Remote Control as a separate mechanism (distinct from channels), matching the docs' "How channels compare" table. - The managed-settings.d/ layering pattern (00-, 10-, 20- prefixed files) is correct for policy settings scope as described in docs.
audit.gaps:
1. CRITICAL: 10-jadecli-channels.json contains: { "$schema": "...", "allowedChannelPlugins": [] } An empty array for allowedChannelPlugins BLOCKS ALL channel plugins per the docs: "When allowedChannelPlugins is set, it replaces the Anthropic allowlist entirely: only the listed plugins can register." The channels documented in INTEGRATIONS.md (telegram, discord, imessage) are therefore blocked by this policy file. This is likely unintentional — either the array should list the approved plugins or the key should be omitted to fall back to the Anthropic default allowlist.
2. channelsEnabled is absent from 10-jadecli-channels.json. Docs state: "On Team and Enterprise plans, channels are off by default... must be true for any channel to deliver messages." Without channelsEnabled: true, all channels are blocked regardless of allowedChannelPlugins.
3. No evidence of --channels flag usage in any startup script, Makefile, or CI config in the repo. INTEGRATIONS.md describes channel capabilities but no launcher script sets up --channels at startup for persistent background use.
4. No fakechat plugin is listed or documented for local development/testing. Docs recommend fakechat as a development channel; absent from INTEGRATIONS.md.
5. Custom webhook channel is mentioned in INTEGRATIONS.md ("Custom webhook | Any | GitHub/CI events → session") but no implementation exists — no plugin source, no webhook receiver script, no reference to channels-reference doc for building one.
6. iMessage channel: INTEGRATIONS.md says "Agent → human notification" but does not document the Full Disk Access requirement (System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access) which the docs flag as required and a common failure point.
7. No pairing workflow documented in INTEGRATIONS.md for Telegram and Discord. Docs require explicit pairing (/telegram:access pair <code>) and policy lock (/telegram:access policy allowlist). These steps are absent from the setup documentation.
8. settings.json does not list fakechat, telegram, discord, or imessage in enabledPlugins — channels must be installed as plugins first. No /plugin install steps are scripted in bootstrap.
audit.actions:
1. FIX IMMEDIATELY: Update bootstrap/managed-settings.d/10-jadecli-channels.json to: { "$schema": "https://json.schemastore.org/claude-code-settings.json", "channelsEnabled": true, "allowedChannelPlugins": [ { "marketplace": "claude-plugins-official", "plugin": "telegram" }, { "marketplace": "claude-plugins-official", "plugin": "discord" }, { "marketplace": "claude-plugins-official", "plugin": "imessage" } ] } This unblocks channels while restricting to only the three approved plugins.
2. ADD a channel launch script (e.g. bootstrap/scripts/start-with-channels.sh): claude --channels plugin:telegram@claude-plugins-official plugin:discord@claude-plugins-official plugin:imessage@claude-plugins-official Document this in INTEGRATIONS.md as the canonical startup command.
3. ADD plugin install steps to bootstrap sequence or a setup script: /plugin install telegram@claude-plugins-official /plugin install discord@claude-plugins-official /plugin install imessage@claude-plugins-official (Or document them in INTEGRATIONS.md step-by-step setup sections.)
4. ADD pairing instructions for Telegram and Discord to INTEGRATIONS.md: Step: DM bot → receive code → run /telegram:access pair <code> → run /telegram:access policy allowlist
5. ADD iMessage Full Disk Access prerequisite to INTEGRATIONS.md: System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access → add Terminal/iTerm
6. ADD fakechat to INTEGRATIONS.md as the local dev/test channel with install + usage steps.
7. DECIDE on custom webhook: either link to channels-reference build guide or remove from INTEGRATIONS.md if not yet implemented.

audit.aligned: - DEVOPS-ALIGNMENT.md Scheduled Runs section documents a three-tier scheduling approach that maps to the docs' scheduling options table: • Session-scoped (docs: /loop) → implied by SessionStart hook rate-limiting • Local machine persistent (docs: Desktop tasks) → described as "Dispatch task from Claude Desktop/Mobile" • Cloud/unattended (docs: Cloud tasks) → "Cowork Scheduled Tasks" with Anthropic-managed cloud - The cron expressions in DEVOPS-ALIGNMENT.md ("0 9 * * *", "0 10 * * 1") are valid 5-field standard cron syntax matching the docs' CronCreate format. - DEVOPS-ALIGNMENT.md documents the correct use case split: cloud tasks for unattended work, desktop/dispatch for local-file tasks — matches docs' tip ("Use cloud tasks for work that should run reliably without your machine. Use Desktop tasks when you need access to local files"). - The --print flag usage in example cron invocations is consistent with headless/non-interactive scheduling patterns.
audit.gaps:
1. DEVOPS-ALIGNMENT.md references "claude --cron" as a CLI flag: claude --cron "0 9 * * *" --print "crawl..." This flag does NOT appear in the scheduled-tasks docs. The native scheduling mechanism documented is /loop (the bundled skill) and the underlying CronCreate/CronList/CronDelete tools — both session-scoped. "claude --cron" may be a non-existent or undocumented flag. Needs verification.
2. DEVOPS-ALIGNMENT.md references "Cowork scheduled tasks": claude cowork --scheduled "daily 9am" "Run plugin ecosystem crawl..." The docs do not document a "cowork" subcommand. The docs refer to "Cloud scheduled tasks" (web-scheduled-tasks) and "Desktop scheduled tasks". "Cowork" is not a documented interface. This may be a misremembered or speculative command.
3. DEVOPS-ALIGNMENT.md references "Dispatch task from Claude Desktop/Mobile" as a scheduling primitive. The docs describe this under "Desktop scheduled tasks" (via Desktop app UI) but DEVOPS-ALIGNMENT.md frames it as a push mechanism ("Send a task to a running local session from your phone"). The actual Desktop scheduling feature (persistent, survives restarts, configurable interval) is not addressed.
4. No /loop usage is documented anywhere in the repo. The docs feature /loop as the primary in-session scheduling primitive with full syntax (e.g. /loop 5m check if deployment finished). DEVOPS-ALIGNMENT.md does not mention /loop at all.
5. No CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_CRON=1 escape hatch is documented. If the scheduler causes interference during a session, there is no documented way to disable it.
6. Three-day expiry of recurring tasks (documented: recurring tasks auto-expire after 3 days) is not addressed in DEVOPS-ALIGNMENT.md. Tasks that should run longer than 3 days need to be implemented as Desktop or Cloud tasks, not session-scoped ones.
7. No actual scheduled task definitions exist in the repo — DEVOPS-ALIGNMENT.md describes intent and example commands but no implementation scripts, Desktop task configs, or Cloud task definitions are present.
8. The docs distinguish minimum intervals: Cloud tasks = 1 hour minimum, Desktop/loop = 1 minute minimum. DEVOPS-ALIGNMENT.md schedules "daily" and "weekly" which are both above Cloud minimum, but "Per session (1x/day)" for config drift check is implemented via SessionStart hook rate-limiting rather than any scheduling primitive — this is architecturally correct but not using the scheduler at all.
audit.actions:
1. VERIFY/FIX "claude --cron" flag: check claude --help output for a --cron flag. If it does not exist, replace the DEVOPS-ALIGNMENT.md examples with the correct /loop syntax or GitHub Actions schedule trigger. Correct in-session syntax: use /loop inside a session, e.g.: /loop 1d crawl code.claude.com/docs and report changes
2. VERIFY/FIX "claude cowork --scheduled": check whether cowork is a real subcommand. If not, replace with the documented alternatives: - Cloud: use the Cloud scheduled tasks UI at claude.ai - Local: use Desktop scheduled tasks via Claude Desktop app - CI: use GitHub Actions schedule trigger (already partially documented in DEVOPS-ALIGNMENT.md)
3. ADD /loop examples to DEVOPS-ALIGNMENT.md for session-scoped polling tasks: /loop 5m check if the deployment finished and tell me what happened /loop 20m review any new PRs opened since last check
4. ADD note on three-day expiry: "Session-scoped /loop tasks expire after 72 hours. For persistent recurring tasks use Desktop or Cloud scheduled tasks."
5. ADD CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_CRON=1 to the env vars reference in settings.json or a .env.example file so operators can disable the scheduler when needed.
6. CONSIDER implementing concrete scheduled task definitions: - A GitHub Actions workflow with schedule trigger for the daily doc crawl (replaces the speculative claude --cron command) - A Desktop task config for weekly MCP registry sync Both are more reliable than session-scoped tasks for the use cases described.
7. CLARIFY "Dispatch" entry in the table: rename to "Desktop scheduled tasks" and link to the Desktop docs. Add that these are persistent (survive restarts) and have 1-minute minimum interval.
